input.old_string:
# zbujb_obliterate_workload -- letter-agnostic, platform-dispatched destroy
# helper. Single greppable verb covering every namespace a prior garrison
# of any letter could have populated. Idempotent on absent state at every
# step; (letter, platform) compatibility is asserted by garrison entry, so
# the helper dispatches on BURN_PLATFORM alone.
#
# Windows scope (one PowerShell session over admin SSH):
#   - Windows SAM entry (net.exe user /delete)
#   - C:\Users\<user>\ Windows profile directory
#   - C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\ Cygwin home
#   - WSL-side /home/<user> via wsl.exe userdel -r inside rbtww-main
# Linux scope: native userdel -r and home purge.
# Mac scope: sysadminctl -deleteUser and home purge.
input.new_string:
# zbujb_obliterate_windows_namespaces WLU -- run the four-namespace
# Windows obliterate sequence as a series of atomic PowerShell calls.
# Each PS call does ONE thing: a probe (emits raw text) or a destructive
# operation (Remove-LocalUser / Remove-Item / wsl.exe userdel). All
# conditional logic lives bash-side via `test ... || { … }` per BCG.
# Probes use only PS cmdlets or wsl.exe with bash-internal `|| true`,
# avoiding the PowerShell native-command stderr-to-terminating-error
# escalation that bites multi-statement absent-tolerant bodies.
zbujb_obliterate_windows_namespaces() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_wlu="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_wlu}" \
    || buc_die "zbujb_obliterate_windows_namespaces: workload user required"

  local z_state

  # SAM entry — Get-LocalUser emits object table if present, empty if
  # absent (SilentlyContinue swallows the not-found error).
  buc_step "    [SAM] Probe Windows local user (${z_wlu})"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "Get-LocalUser -Name '${z_wlu}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" \
      > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "SAM probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
  z_state="${z_state//[$'\r\n\t ']/}"
  test -z "${z_state}" || {
    buc_step "    [SAM] Remove Windows local user (${z_wlu})"
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-LocalUser -Name '${z_wlu}'" \
        > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
        2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
      || buc_die "Remove-LocalUser failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  }

  # Windows profile directory.
  buc_step "    [Profile] Probe C:\\Users\\${z_wlu}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "Test-Path 'C:\\Users\\${z_wlu}'" \
      > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "Profile-dir probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
  z_state="${z_state//[$'\r\n']/}"
  case "${z_state}" in
    True)
      buc_step "    [Profile] Remove C:\\Users\\${z_wlu}"
      zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force 'C:\\Users\\${z_wlu}'" \
          > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
          2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
        || buc_die "Remove C:\\Users\\${z_wlu} failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
      ;;
    False)
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Test-Path on C:\\Users\\${z_wlu} returned unexpected: '${z_state}'"
      ;;
  esac

  # Cygwin home directory.
  buc_step "    [Cygwin] Probe C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "Test-Path 'C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu}'" \
      > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "Cygwin-home probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
  z_state="${z_state//[$'\r\n']/}"
  case "${z_state}" in
    True)
      buc_step "    [Cygwin] Remove C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu}"
      zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force 'C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu}'" \
          > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
          2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
        || buc_die "Remove C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu} failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
      ;;
    False)
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Test-Path on C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu} returned unexpected: '${z_state}'"
      ;;
  esac

  # WSL distribution presence — case-scan stripped output for the
  # canonical name (preflight already verified, but stay defensive).
  buc_step "    [WSL] Probe distribution list"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --list --quiet 2>\$null" \
      > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "WSL distro probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
  z_state="${z_state//$'\r'/}"
  case $'\n'"${z_state}"$'\n' in
    *$'\n'"${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"$'\n'*)
      # WSL user probe: bash-inside-wsl handles getent's absent-state
      # via `|| true` so wsl.exe still exits 0 when user is absent
      # (avoids the wrapper's exit-code propagation tripping).
      buc_step "    [WSL] Probe Linux user (${z_wlu}) inside ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
      zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -c \"getent passwd '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true\"" \
          > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
          2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
        || buc_die "WSL user probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
      z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
      z_state="${z_state//[$'\r\n']/}"
      test -z "${z_state}" || {
        buc_step "    [WSL] Remove Linux user (${z_wlu}) inside ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
        zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root userdel -r '${z_wlu}'" \
            > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
            2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
          || buc_die "WSL userdel failed for ${z_wlu} — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
      }
      ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_obliterate_workload -- letter-agnostic, platform-dispatched destroy
# helper. Single greppable verb covering every namespace a prior garrison
# of any letter could have populated. Idempotent on absent state at every
# step; (letter, platform) compatibility is asserted by garrison entry, so
# the helper dispatches on BURN_PLATFORM alone.
#
# Windows scope (one PowerShell session per atomic operation):
#   - Windows SAM entry (Remove-LocalUser if Get-LocalUser found it)
#   - C:\Users\<user>\ Windows profile directory (Remove-Item if Test-Path)
#   - C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\ Cygwin home (Remove-Item if Test-Path)
#   - WSL-side /home/<user> via wsl.exe userdel -r (gated on rbtww-main
#     present and getent passwd hit). Decisions live bash-side; PS is
#     atomic. See zbujb_obliterate_windows_namespaces.

toolUseResult.oldString:
# zbujb_obliterate_workload -- letter-agnostic, platform-dispatched destroy
# helper. Single greppable verb covering every namespace a prior garrison
# of any letter could have populated. Idempotent on absent state at every
# step; (letter, platform) compatibility is asserted by garrison entry, so
# the helper dispatches on BURN_PLATFORM alone.
#
# Windows scope (one PowerShell session over admin SSH):
#   - Windows SAM entry (net.exe user /delete)
#   - C:\Users\<user>\ Windows profile directory
#   - C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\ Cygwin home
#   - WSL-side /home/<user> via wsl.exe userdel -r inside rbtww-main
# Linux scope: native userdel -r and home purge.
# Mac scope: sysadminctl -deleteUser and home purge.
toolUseResult.newString:
# zbujb_obliterate_windows_namespaces WLU -- run the four-namespace
# Windows obliterate sequence as a series of atomic PowerShell calls.
# Each PS call does ONE thing: a probe (emits raw text) or a destructive
# operation (Remove-LocalUser / Remove-Item / wsl.exe userdel). All
# conditional logic lives bash-side via `test ... || { … }` per BCG.
# Probes use only PS cmdlets or wsl.exe with bash-internal `|| true`,
# avoiding the PowerShell native-command stderr-to-terminating-error
# escalation that bites multi-statement absent-tolerant bodies.
zbujb_obliterate_windows_namespaces() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_wlu="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_wlu}" \
    || buc_die "zbujb_obliterate_windows_namespaces: workload user required"

  local z_state

  # SAM entry — Get-LocalUser emits object table if present, empty if
  # absent (SilentlyContinue swallows the not-found error).
  buc_step "    [SAM] Probe Windows local user (${z_wlu})"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "Get-LocalUser -Name '${z_wlu}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" \
      > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "SAM probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
  z_state="${z_state//[$'\r\n\t ']/}"
  test -z "${z_state}" || {
    buc_step "    [SAM] Remove Windows local user (${z_wlu})"
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-LocalUser -Name '${z_wlu}'" \
        > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
        2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
      || buc_die "Remove-LocalUser failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  }

  # Windows profile directory.
  buc_step "    [Profile] Probe C:\\Users\\${z_wlu}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "Test-Path 'C:\\Users\\${z_wlu}'" \
      > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "Profile-dir probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
  z_state="${z_state//[$'\r\n']/}"
  case "${z_state}" in
    True)
      buc_step "    [Profile] Remove C:\\Users\\${z_wlu}"
      zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force 'C:\\Users\\${z_wlu}'" \
          > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
          2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
        || buc_die "Remove C:\\Users\\${z_wlu} failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
      ;;
    False)
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Test-Path on C:\\Users\\${z_wlu} returned unexpected: '${z_state}'"
      ;;
  esac

  # Cygwin home directory.
  buc_step "    [Cygwin] Probe C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "Test-Path 'C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu}'" \
      > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "Cygwin-home probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
  z_state="${z_state//[$'\r\n']/}"
  case "${z_state}" in
    True)
      buc_step "    [Cygwin] Remove C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu}"
      zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force 'C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu}'" \
          > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
          2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
        || buc_die "Remove C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu} failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
      ;;
    False)
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Test-Path on C:\\cygwin64\\home\\${z_wlu} returned unexpected: '${z_state}'"
      ;;
  esac

  # WSL distribution presence — case-scan stripped output for the
  # canonical name (preflight already verified, but stay defensive).
  buc_step "    [WSL] Probe distribution list"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --list --quiet 2>\$null" \
      > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "WSL distro probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
  z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
  z_state="${z_state//$'\r'/}"
  case $'\n'"${z_state}"$'\n' in
    *$'\n'"${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"$'\n'*)
      # WSL user probe: bash-inside-wsl handles getent's absent-state
      # via `|| true` so wsl.exe still exits 0 when user is absent
      # (avoids the wrapper's exit-code propagation tripping).
      buc_step "    [WSL] Probe Linux user (${z_wlu}) inside ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
      zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -c \"getent passwd '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true\"" \
          > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
          2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
        || buc_die "WSL user probe failed — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
      z_state=$(<"${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}")
      z_state="${z_state//[$'\r\n']/}"
      test -z "${z_state}" || {
        buc_step "    [WSL] Remove Linux user (${z_wlu}) inside ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
        zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root userdel -r '${z_wlu}'" \
            > "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT}" \
            2> "${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}" \
          || buc_die "WSL userdel failed for ${z_wlu} — see ${ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR}"
      }
      ;;
  esac
}

content.text: Cmd.exe nested-quote tokenization issue: my `bash -c "..."` pattern injects double quotes inside the PowerShell command-line, which breaks cmd's quote pairing. Fix is to use PS single-quoted strings (with `''` for embedded singles) for the bash argument so no double quotes appear inside the cmd-level token.

content.text:
Stopping at the new failure point. Diagnosis so far:

The split-atom obliterate worked end-to-end — all 4 probes ran clean, decisions made bash-side, no PS escalation. Garrison then proceeded:
- Step 3 (Create workload) — returned success
- Step 4 (Place workload trust at `/mnt/c/Users/bujuw_user/.ssh/authorized_keys`) — succeeded; note the path message is now correct
- Step 5 (Plant workload privkey at `/home/bujuw_user/.ssh/id_ed25519`) — failed with `install: invalid user 'bujuw_user'`

Step 5 fails because `install -m 600 -o 'bujuw_user' -g 'bujuw_user' …` can't resolve the user inside WSL — meaning the WSL-side Linux user doesn't actually exist at step 5 time. But step 3 reported success, and step 3's heredoc has `set -euo pipefail` and runs `useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash 'bujuw_user'` inside `wsl.exe --user root bash -s`.

Two main hypotheses:

**A. Step 3's `useradd` actually failed but `set -e` in the heredoc didn't catch it because `wsl.exe` swallowed the non-zero exit.** This would be a real wsl.exe-bash interaction bug we'd need to defend against. Test: probe `/etc/passwd` inside `rbtww-main` right now and see if `bujuw_user` is present.

**B. Step 3 succeeded creating the user, but something between step 3 and step 5 dropped them.** Less likely — nothing else in the ceremony touches WSL users.

This is unrelated to the obliterate work — it's a pre-existing issue in step 3 that was masked previously when fenestrate/garrison hadn't been exercised end-to-end.

Want me to:
1. **Probe** — run `tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh bujn-winpc 'wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root cat /etc/passwd'` to see whether `bujuw_user` actually got created. Decides between A and B.
2. **Or** propose a fix without further probing.

Notch landed (commit `5e677ec6`). Resume prompt for a clean chat:

---

```
mount A-

The pace ₢A-AAv (correct-wsl-user-model) hit a step-5 failure during operator-side end-to-end exercise of garrison-w against bujn-winpc. The in-tree work for the pace is committed (the latest three commits — workload-user rename, obliterate-helper introduction, split-atom Windows obliterate); only the end-to-end exercise remains as a "done when" item. Don't redo the in-tree work.

What happened in the last run:

- Steps 1, 2, 3, 4 reported success. The new split-atom obliterate (zbujb_obliterate_windows_namespaces) ran clean across SAM, Windows profile, Cygwin home, WSL distro+user — each PowerShell call is one atom (probe-emits-raw-text or destructive-op), bash-side carries all conditional logic via test/case + || buc_die per BCG.
- Step 5 (plant workload privkey via wsl.exe --user root bash heredoc) failed: `install: invalid user 'bujuw_user'`. Step 5 runs `install -m 600 -o 'bujuw_user' -g 'bujuw_user' "${ztmp}" "${z_target}"` inside WSL, which can't resolve the user — meaning the WSL Linux user doesn't exist at step 5 time even though step 3's heredoc reported success. Step 3's heredoc:

  set -euo pipefail
  net.exe user '...' /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes > /dev/null
  useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '...'
  passwd  --lock '...'

  is invoked via `zbujb_admin_exec w` (= wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root bash -s).

Two hypotheses to discriminate:

A) Step 3's useradd actually failed but wsl.exe swallowed the inner bash non-zero exit, masking it from the heredoc's set -e. This would be a real wsl.exe-bash interop bug to defend against.
B) User created successfully but vanished between step 3 and step 5. Less likely — nothing else in the ceremony touches WSL users.

Resume plan:

1) Verify rocket (the bujn-winpc host) is reachable. If not, the pace stays paused.
2) Probe /etc/passwd inside rbtww-main:

   tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh bujn-winpc 'wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root cat /etc/passwd'

   This last attempt timed out at SSH connect. If it works now, look for bujuw_user.
3) Discriminate A vs B and pick a fix. If A, harden step 3 to assert post-useradd that the user is in /etc/passwd before returning. If B, investigate the intervening operations.
4) Re-run tt/buw-jpGw.GarrisonWsl.sh bujn-winpc to confirm step 5 lands.
5) When all 6 garrison steps pass end-to-end, ask the user before wrapping ₢A-AAv.

Constraints:
- Don't loop on PowerShell experimentation. Try, diagnose, explain, get approval before next cycle.
- Don't re-touch the in-tree code/spec work for the namespace-honest verb amendment — it is settled and shipped in the latest commits.
- bujb_jurisdiction.sh kindle constants ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT/STDERR are the buc_die-referenced forensic slots (preserved per BCG, last call's content visible).
```
